finding_results.notes: Paul's panic now registers through claims-language self-surveillance and the concealment actions themselves. The passage keeps the mouth wipe, surface wipe, and wash-up without pausing to argue that he is innocent or that the logic is merely broken.
The dawn pass now lands the crooked study print, missing orange bottle, and thinned bathroom shelf, then moves to a single synthesis of what those changes mean before the drying-rack break.
Only one refrigerator reference and one loon call remain, both in the same early room-silence beat. Later recurring punctuations were removed.
Joel's first fireplace-chair image remains in full. The second mention is compressed to a brief continuity reminder without re-describing posture or replaying the sleep-versus-performance read.
Em-dash usage is now limited to five interruption/self-correction dialogue lines. Verified chapter-wide at 5 em-dash lines out of 101 prose lines (5.0%).
Sentence openings were diversified across the CPR, tampering, overnight-listening, and dawn sections. Verified chapter-wide at 1 'He' opener out of 327 sentences (0.3%).

Paul interrupts Joel twice (lines 79, 83) — once cutting off Joel's initial reasoning, once completing Joel's sentence before Joel can land it. The practical stakes remain legible: ambulance delay (Tommy, line 73), deputy/statements (Joel, line 77), same outcome at 7 AM (Joel, line 81). No single character delivers the full case in one block. Narrator description of Joel's voice trimmed to remove admiring qualifier ('each word placed and weighed before delivery'). Contraction levels verified: Tommy high (gonna, he's, it's), Joel variable (contractions mixed with precision), Paul moderate (still, have to, don't).

The later concealment beats now ride on sound, room-state, object absence, and physical staging rather than explicit insurance abstractions.

pass_summaries.summary: All packet findings were addressed in place. The chapter now lets Paul's concealment read without exculpatory narration, lands the dawn state change with one clean synthesis before the drying-rack close, and clears the motif, em-dash, Joel-repeat, and sentence-opener thresholds.

Revised the 911-delay exchange (lines 69-85) to fragment Joel's polished legal brief across three interrupted turns, with Tommy and Paul exerting active friction on the reasoning. The practical stakes (ambulance distance, deputy timing, identical outcome for Martin) remain legible but now arrive through messy overlap rather than a single briefed diagnosis. All other dialogue in the chapter was audited for contraction compliance and voice fidelity against the style bible; no additional issues found. No continuity changes introduced.
Revised chapter_02 to preserve Paul's insurance-shaped precision without making claims vocabulary the default explanatory layer, especially across the middle-to-late concealment sequence.
